reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
Early June. A Tuesday. Nothing about today is going to make a highlight reel. You'll answer emails, you'll eat something at your desk, you'll maybe go for a walk when the light turns golden around seven. This is the part nobody warned you about — that most of sobriety isn't dramatic. It's just Tuesdays.

When I was 22 and white-knuckling weekends, I thought a sober life would feel like punishment stretched across decades of identical days. What I didn't understand is that drinking was the thing flattening my days, not saving them. Every Sunday was triage. Every Monday was apology. I had no Tuesdays — I had hangovers wearing a Tuesday costume. The ordinary day, the one where nothing is on fire and you can actually feel the breeze and remember what you said yesterday, was the thing I had traded away.

Durant's line about habit is usually quoted in service of ambition, but I think it works better in reverse: you become whoever you keep showing up as. Show up sober on a forgettable Tuesday in June and you're casting a vote for the person who gets to go skiing next winter, who gets to be trusted by their friends, who gets to have a life with a through-line. The big stuff is downstream of this. Today is the stuff.
reflection.meditation: Where in today — this specific Tuesday — can I be fully present instead of waiting for something more interesting to arrive?

affirmations: I am building a life on ordinary days, not in spite of them.
Today I get to remember what I do, say, and feel — and that's a gift I almost gave away.
I am someone my future self is counting on, and I'm showing up.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a quiet Tuesday I can actually feel. Grateful for the people who stayed while I figured this out, and for the version of me who, on some unremarkable day, decided to try. The small things — coffee that tastes like coffee, a clear head at 4pm, a phone I'm not afraid to check — these used to be impossible. Today they're just Tuesday.
gratitude.closing: One day. This one. That's the whole job.
